stories.headline: Gatchalian elected Senate President, settling weeks-long leadership dispute
stories.summary: Senator Sherwin Gatchalian was elected Senate President during a special session on June 17, securing the support of 13 senators after Senator Joel Villanueva joined his bloc to meet the required majority. Gatchalian becomes the fourth person to hold the post in the 20th Congress, replacing Alan Peter Cayetano, who was removed on June 3. Senator Vicente Sotto III was elected Senate President pro tempore. In his acceptance remarks, Gatchalian said he would listen to and cooperate with colleagues across political lines.
stories.context: The Senate had been split for weeks between blocs led by Cayetano and Gatchalian after the June 3 reshuffle, complicating floor proceedings and the response to President Marcos's special session call.

stories.headline: Congress convenes special session on Marcos priority bills and pending appointments
stories.summary: Both chambers of Congress convened on June 17 for a special session called by President Marcos under Proclamation No. 1318 to act on priority legislation and allow the Commission on Appointments to confirm pending Cabinet, military, and diplomatic appointments delayed during the Senate leadership row. The Senate also adopted a resolution seeking adequate government funding for recovery from the June 8 Mindanao earthquake. Marcos cited eight priority measures, including bills on student assistance, child nutrition, geriatric health, and an anti-political dynasty proposal.

stories.headline: Sara Duterte's defense denies all impeachment charges ahead of June 18 pre-trial
stories.summary: Vice President Sara Duterte's legal team submitted a pre-trial brief to the Senate impeachment court denying all four articles of impeachment, describing the case as legally deficient and unsupported by credible evidence. The defense said it plans to present witnesses from agencies including the Commission on Audit, the Department of Justice, and the National Bureau of Investigation. The charges cover alleged misuse of confidential funds, undisclosed assets in her wealth declarations, and contested public statements. Duterte has called the case politically motivated.
stories.context: The House impeached Duterte in May 2026. The Senate impeachment court, presided over by Sherwin Gatchalian, set a pre-trial conference for June 18, with the trial proper scheduled to begin July 6.
stories.watch_for: The pre-trial conference is set for June 18 and will not be open to the media or public; the trial proper is scheduled for July 6.

stories.headline: Navy says 26 Chinese vessels monitored in West Philippine Sea over a week
stories.summary: The Philippine Navy said it tracked 26 Chinese vessels in the West Philippine Sea from June 9 to 15, down from 41 the previous week. The count included 19 China Coast Guard ships and seven People's Liberation Army Navy vessels spread across Ayungin Shoal, Bajo de Masinloc, Escoda Shoal, and the Pag-asa Islands, according to the Navy. The military regularly reports such tallies as part of its monitoring of Chinese activity in contested waters.

stories.headline: Magnitude 4.6 aftershock jolts Sarangani amid quake recovery
stories.summary: A magnitude 4.6 aftershock struck off Sarangani at 4:03 p.m. on June 17, with an epicenter about 50 kilometers southwest of Balut Island at a depth of 25 kilometers, the Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology said. The agency recorded Intensity IV in Kiamba and Maasim and lower intensities in nearby areas, and said no damage was expected. It was among continuing aftershocks of the magnitude 7.8 earthquake that struck off Sarangani on June 8.
stories.context: The June 8 magnitude 7.8 quake killed dozens and damaged tens of thousands of homes across southern Mindanao, prompting states of calamity and an ongoing government relief effort.

stories.headline: Kanlaon Volcano logs repeated ash emissions; Alert Level 2 maintained
stories.summary: The Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology said Kanlaon Volcano in Negros Island produced 15 ash emission events since June 15, generating plumes 100 to 500 meters above the crater that drifted generally southwest. Its 24-hour monitoring also logged 18 volcanic earthquakes, including 11 tremors. Alert Level 2 remains in effect, indicating a moderate level of unrest, and the agency reminded the public to stay out of the four-kilometer permanent danger zone.
stories.context: Phivolcs has said ash emissions are expected at Alert Level 2 and do not necessarily signal an imminent eruption.

stories.headline: PAGASA: No tropical cyclone in PAR as easterlies bring scattered rains
stories.summary: PAGASA reported no tropical cyclone within the Philippine area of responsibility on June 17, with easterlies affecting Southern Luzon, the Visayas, and the eastern section of Mindanao. The weather bureau said Calabarzon, Mimaropa, the Bicol Region, the Visayas, and parts of Mindanao could see isolated rainshowers or thunderstorms, while Metro Manila and the rest of the country would have partly cloudy skies with possible localized thunderstorms. It warned of possible flash floods or landslides during severe thunderstorms.

stories.headline: Economists split on size of expected BSP rate hike at June 18 meeting
stories.summary: Most economists surveyed expect the Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas to raise its policy rate at its June 18 meeting, though they are divided on the size, according to Manila Bulletin. Six of nine analysts polled saw a 25-basis-point increase to 4.75 percent, while HSBC and Standard Chartered favored a larger move. The central bank is weighing inflation, which peaked at 7.2 percent in April and eased to 6.8 percent in May, against first-quarter economic growth that slowed to 2.8 percent, its weakest in five years.
stories.watch_for: The BSP Monetary Board announces its rate decision on June 18.

stories.headline: Diesel and kerosene prices fall in June 16 fuel adjustment; gasoline mixed
stories.summary: Oil companies implemented fuel price changes effective 6 a.m. on June 16, with diesel down by P3.71 to P5.71 per liter and kerosene down by P0.50 to P2.50 per liter, according to GMA News Online. Gasoline movements were mixed, ranging from a rollback of up to P0.32 to an increase of up to P1.68 per liter. The Department of Energy said adjustments could vary by retailer and location, and the changes partly reversed increases seen the previous week.

stories.headline: Volleyball Nations League women's leg opens in Pasig
stories.summary: The women's Volleyball Nations League returns to the Philippines with its second week of matches running June 17 to 21 at the Philsports Arena in Pasig, the first time the women's tournament has been held in the country in four years. The leg features Italy, the United States, Japan, Czechia, the Dominican Republic, and Serbia. The Philippines is not fielding a team in the competition.

stories.headline: G7 pledges stronger Ukraine air defenses and tighter Russia sanctions
stories.summary: Leaders at the Group of Seven summit in France pledged to increase deliveries of air defense systems and interceptors to Ukraine and to consider licensing it to produce its own equipment, Al Jazeera reported. The bloc also said it would tighten sanctions on Russia's oil and gas sectors. Leaders welcomed the US-Iran agreement, and France and the United Kingdom offered support for resuming maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, including mine clearance.

stories.headline: Africa CDC warns DR Congo Ebola outbreak could become worst on record
stories.summary: The head of the Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention warned that an Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo could become deadlier than the worst on record unless contained soon, Al Jazeera reported. Authorities have recorded 837 confirmed cases and 196 deaths in the DRC, with 19 cases and two deaths in neighboring Uganda. The outbreak, caused by the Bundibugyo strain for which there is no approved vaccine or treatment, faces challenges including more than 26,000 untraced contacts and a large funding shortfall.
stories.context: The World Health Organization declared the outbreak a public health emergency of international concern in May 2026. The worst previous Ebola epidemic, in West Africa, killed more than 11,000 people.

stories.headline: Bank of Japan raises key rate to 1%, highest since 1995
stories.summary: The Bank of Japan raised its benchmark interest rate by 25 basis points to 1 percent following a two-day board meeting, its highest level since 1995, Nikkei Asia reported. The central bank cited the need to stay ahead of inflation, with consumer prices running above its 2 percent target, and pointed to uncertainty from the Middle East conflict. Deputy Governor Shinichi Uchida led the meeting as Governor Kazuo Ueda was hospitalized.
